MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Atlas Heating And Ventilating Company, Ltd.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-03-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gilliam F. Stewart of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Atlas Hearing and Ventilating Company, Ltd.
Patrick R. Campbell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties John Pelleriti and Faye Pelleriti

RULING:
DEFENDANT ATLAS HEATING AND VENTILATING COMPANY, LTD.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Atlas Heating and Ventilating Company, Ltd.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to defendant's argument regarding proper identification of defendant and its employees, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the Atlas entity at issue is this defendant. Plaintiffs' interrogatory responses, the deposition testimony of Mr. Pelleriti and the Contractors State License Board's license details for other Atlas entities is insufficient to sustain defendant's burden. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Pelleriti's deposition testimony and declaration, the deposition testimony of Theresa Meyer and the Atlas websites would create a triable issue whether the Atlas entity at issue is this defendant. As to defendant's duty arguments, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it owed no duty to Mr. Pelleriti. Defendant's arguments based on the doctrine of primary assumption of risk and the sophisticated user doctrine/defense rely on the premise that Mr. Pelleriti was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant only after his training regarding asbestos products and the hazards of asbestos. However defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that premise so it failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it owed no duty to Mr. Pelleriti under either or both theories. Mr. Pelleriti's deposition testimony precludes defendant from sustaining that burden. However even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Pelleriti's deposition testimony and declaration would create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (fireproofing) attributable to defendant only after his training regarding asbestos products and the hazards of asbestos. Further, as to defendant's argument based on the sophisticated user doctrine/defense, even if defendant had demonstrated that it could apply, summary judgment would be unwarranted because it is not a complete defense. It would only preclude plaintiffs' claims based on failure to warn, which defendant did not demonstrate were the sole basis of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to Mr. Stewart's declaration and Exhibits F, G, H and I (Atlas entity license details) are overruled. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' Exhibits H, I, J and K (deposition excerpts prior to defendant being a party) are sustained and its objections to paragraph 9 of Mr. Pelleriti's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
